title: Food Scientists and Technologists
description: Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food.

tasks.text: Inspect food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, quality, and waste management.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Check raw ingredients for maturity or stability for processing, and finished products for safety, quality, and nutritional value.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition, flavor, color, texture, nutritional value, and convenience.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop food standards and production specifications, safety and sanitary regulations, and waste management and water supply specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stay up to date on new regulations and current events regarding food science by reviewing scientific literature.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Study the structure and composition of food or the changes foods undergo in storage and processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with process engineers, plant operators, flavor experts, and packaging and marketing specialists to resolve problems in product development.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test new products for flavor, texture, color, nutritional content, and adherence to government and industry standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop new food items for production, based on consumer feedback.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop new or improved ways of preserving, processing, packaging, storing, and delivering foods, using knowledge of chemistry, microbiology, and other sciences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate food processing and storage operations and assist in the development of quality assurance programs for such operations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Seek substitutes for harmful or undesirable additives, such as nitrites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
